mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of rising rate periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals economic weakness that triggers central bank easing expectations, lifting equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of triggered instances across any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction from price shocks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows when both inflation acceleration and sales deceleration align.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity that supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of early-cycle observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year drop in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and credit conditions, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered months across any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of divergence periods over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs for corporations and households, immediately boosting risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of rate-cut periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative returns in more than 60 percent of high-growth quarters across a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation.
